mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the unemployment rate above 8.5% over 3 months signals deteriorating labor market conditions, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns as investor confidence weakens.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite rising unemployment.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in inflation (CPIAUCSL) and low interest rates (FEDFUNDS) create stagflationary risks, causing equity market volatility and negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 50% of instances where CPIAUCSL > 230 and FEDFUNDS < 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts (HOUST) combined with rising consumer confidence (RSAFS) indicate robust demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns during post-recession recovery phases.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 40% of instances where HOUST > 700k and RSAFS > 400k.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects expanding manufacturing activity, which historically correlates with positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to correlate with SPX 5d returns (Rank IC < 0.05) over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll gains exceeding 130k in a month signal a tightening labor market, which historically drives positive S&P 500 returns as corporate earnings prospects improve.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 50% of instances where PAYEMS > 130k.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP (GDPC1) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates strong demand-side momentum, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 40% of instances where GDPC1 > 13500 and HOUST > 700k.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 230 combined with tightening monetary policy (FEDFUNDS > 0.5%) signals reduced equity valuations, causing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances where CPIAUCSL > 230 and FEDFUNDS > 0.5%.
